{{Infobox event
|name=The Mouse becomes distressed when Alice mentions cats, revealing his family's hatred of them
|participants=Alice, Mouse
|chapitre=2
}}

== Course of events ==

In the course of her extraordinary adventures, Alice encounters the Mouse at a point when she has been reduced to a very small size through her interaction with various mysterious objects. During this encounter with the Mouse, Alice mentions cats. This reference produces an immediate and pronounced distress in the Mouse, who reacts strongly to the mention of felines.

The Mouse's acute reaction to Alice's mention of cats reveals a significant fact about his family background: his family maintains a deep and abiding hatred of cats. The intensity of his distress demonstrates that this familial animosity toward cats is a matter of considerable emotional weight for the Mouse himself. The revelation of this family characteristic provides insight into the Mouse's personal history and the values held within his household.

== References ==

- Alice's Adventures in Wonderland

[[Category:Événements]]
